title: Garlicky Korean Kimchi Mitbanchan
description: A rich and creamy mitbanchan dish from Busan, Korea, made with garlicky kimchi and a strong white flour temper.

ingredients.name: Kimchi (store-bought or homemade)
ingredients.notes: Chopped or finely chopped kimchi is best for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Strong White Flour
ingredients.notes: Use a high-protein flour to ensure a smooth temper.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Unrefined sea salt or kosher salt works well.

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Use a mild sugar to balance the flavors.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic to release its flavors.

ingredients.name: Gochujang (Korean chili paste)
ingredients.notes: Adjust to your desired level of spiciness.

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality sesame oil for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Green onions
ingredients.notes: Finely chopped green onions add a nice texture.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Adjust the amount of water to achieve the right temper.

instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine kimchi, flour, salt, sugar, garlic, and gochujang. Blend until smooth.
Add sesame oil and blend until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a saucepan and add water. Whisk until smooth.
Heat the mixture over medium heat, whisking constantly, until it reaches 190°F (88°C).
Remove from heat and stir in green onions.
Let the temper cool to 160°F (71°C) before serving.
Serve warm or at room temperature, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
